user_message: Working on Humongous Insurance's QBR preparation. Got the meeting transcript -- help me create the summary.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-19 16:16 EST
Subject: Re: QBR preparation Discussion - Next Steps

Just checking in on the QBR preparation proposal. Our IT Director is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
Date: 2024-02-14 12:57 EST
Subject: Follow-up: QBR preparation Review

There's been a change in our timeline for QBR preparation. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-03 13:40 EST
Subject: URGENT: QBR preparation Issue at Humongous Insurance

There's been a change in our timeline for QBR preparation. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Humongous Insurance: the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF42-K trap (Opportunity hypothesis dismissed when faint-but-real signal warranted exploratio); produces complete Signal assessment, opportunity hypothesis with confidence, ownership routing, response draft, next-s
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF42-K failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF42-K failure mode (Opportunity hypothesis dismissed when faint-but-real signal warranted exploratio)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct QBR preparation decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF42-K -- Opportunity hypothesis dismissed when faint-but-real signal warranted exploratio
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
